title: Ch 9.2 — Multiple Catch & Finally: The Evil ATM
description:
Practice catching multiple exception types and using finally for cleanup.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

The Bank of Evil ATM processes withdrawal requests. Each request
provides an account ID (String) and an amount (String). Several
things can go wrong:

  • The amount might not be a valid number  → NumberFormatException
  • The account ID might be null            → NullPointerException
  • The amount might be negative or zero    → IllegalArgumentException

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Your Task: complete processTransaction()
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Method signature (already provided):
    public String processTransaction(String accountId, String amountText)

  Inside this method, add a try-catch-finally block:

  TRY block:
    1. Call accountId.toUpperCase() to force a NullPointerException
       if accountId is null. Store the result in a local variable.
    2. Parse amountText to a double using Double.parseDouble().
    3. If the parsed amount <= 0, throw:
         new IllegalArgumentException("Amount must be positive")
    4. If all checks pass, set result = "Dispensing $" + amount
         + " for account " + uppercaseId

  CATCH blocks (in this order):
    • NumberFormatException  → set result = "Error: invalid amount '" + amountText + "'"
    • NullPointerException   → set result = "Error: account ID cannot be null"
    • IllegalArgumentException → set result = "Error: " + e.getMessage()

  FINALLY block:
    • Append " | Transaction logged." to result
      (result += " | Transaction logged.")

  Return result.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
IMPORTANT DETAILS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Declare  String result = "";  before the try block so it is
  in scope for the finally block and the return statement.
• The finally block always runs — even when an exception occurs.
• Return result AFTER the try-catch-finally (not inside it).

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Double.parseDouble("abc") throws NumberFormatException.
• Calling null.toUpperCase() throws NullPointerException.
• You throw IllegalArgumentException yourself with the throw keyword.
